title: GuardDuty Malware Protection for S3 - Overview and Demo | Amazon Web Services
description:
Using GuardDuty, you can now identify new objects uploaded with malware from untrusted sources. GuardDuty will monitor new objects on the configured S3 bucket, detect for malware, apply tags, and will provide event notification so you can quarantine the infected objects.

Documentation:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/guardduty/latest/ug/gdu-malware-protection-s3.html

Learn more at: https://go.aws/3Xv7oYI

title: Query your Data Lake with Natural Language | Amazon Web Services
description:
In this video, you'll learn how to deploy a demo that enables you to query your data lake using natural language. The demo showcases an end-to-end workflow that automates the process of searching a data catalog, generating SQL queries, executing those queries, and providing natural language responses based on the query results.

Get the code: https://go.aws/3VzKGMr

This video explains how to deploy a web interface where you can ask questions like "Who are the top 5 customers who bought from French suppliers?", and get answers based on your data. Walk through how it:
1) searches the data catalog for relevant tables
2) generates SQL queries
3) executes against the data lake
4) provides results in plain English

The video covers deploying on AWS Cloud9, launching the Streamlit app, and indexing sample catalogs. Get insights into the implementation using vector search and generative AI models. Whether building data exploration tools or conversational data interfaces, this demo is a powerful starting point to extend with your own data sources.

title: Datadog used AWS IAM Access Analyzer to review and remediate identity risks | Amazon Web Services
description:
Datadog, a SaaS-based unified observability and security platform company, provides Cloud Security Management (CSM) that offers abilities to review and resolve risks due to privilege escalations, permission gaps, and cross-account access. Datadog CSM uses AWS IAM Access Analyzer to further improve the detection of permission gaps to provide right-sized policy recommendations

Learn more about Datadog CSM and AWS IAM Access Analyzer at https://go.aws/3XObICD

title: Create an Amazon WorkSpaces Bring Your Own License (BYOL) image with Hyper-V | Amazon Web Services
description:
In this video, I show you how to create and export an Amazon WorkSpaces and Bring Your Own License (BYOL) image. We will go over essential settings for the virtual machine and Windows that will give you the best opportunity for success in exporting and then importing this image into Amazon WorkSpaces.

Admin Guide: https://go.aws/4erfnMf
Blog Site: https://go.aws/4cuJ7q2
Create an Amazon WorkSpaces (BYOL) image with VMware Workstation: https://go.aws/4exFt08
Best practices for successful BYOL image creation for Amazon WorkSpaces: https://go.aws/3Xz8S43

title: Explore how the NFL season schedule is powered by Amazon EC2 Spot Instances | Amazon Web Services
description:
The NFL has used 400,000 Amazon EC2 Spot Instances to develop the season schedule over the last 10 years, saving over $20 million in the process. Check out some of the #Spot Instances best practices the #NFL leverages to do this successfully year over year.

Learn more about Amazon EC2 Spot Instances: https://go.aws/4emTBcy
